steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank appears within a relative clause interrupted by an opening em-dash after that. The phrase “through community involvement and scientific ______” is a prepositional phrase with a compound noun object (pairing “community involvement” and “scientific study”). All choices complete it with the noun study.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Em-dashes used to set off parenthetical information must come in pairs; choice D correctly uses study— to close the opening em-dash and properly enclose the phrase “through community involvement and scientific study.” Choices A, B, and C violate this rule: A leaves the opening em-dash unclosed, B uses a comma that doesn't match, and C uses a colon that doesn't match. That matches choice D — D is correct.
